description:
Goal: execute compiler2 macro functions as IR-lowered compile-time Fz code over the same Fz-shaped quoted AST values produced by the parser and consumed by source production, without the old `CompileTimeEvaluator`, `exec::Value` AST codec, or repeated AST format conversion.

Signal:
- a local `defmacro inc(x) do quote do: unquote(x) + 1 end end` lowers to interpreter-runnable IR, runs during source production, and returns a new AST root in the same source heap
- macro functions receive `__CALLER__` plus quoted argument roots as ordinary Fz values (`AnyValueRef` roots to tuples/lists/atoms/maps/etc.); args are not evaluated and are not decoded to Rust AST first
- macro return values are `AnyValueRef` roots in the active compile-time process heap; recursive expansion consumes the returned root directly and derives source identity before publishing facts
- macros can call `Fz.Compiler` service externs with AST roots, allowing compiler services to save function AST/source facts from the current process heap
- quote/unquote lower as compile-time source-value construction/splice operations over the compiler2 source schema; they do not call the old AST codec and do not allocate into a second AST representation
- quote/unquote allocate and splice values in that heap, preserving lexical context metadata
- macro execution errors surface through compiler2 diagnostics/telemetry with call-site and definition context
- no fz2 path calls `frontend::macros::expand_program_with_types`, `exec::ast_value`, or the scratch `CompileTimeEvaluator`

Change:
- add a macro-executable fact/entry path: `defmacro` source facts lower to IR before the macro can be invoked
- run macro calls through `ir_interp` on the active compile-time `Process`; the process supplies the heap/world, so the Fz-visible ABI passes roots, not heap handles
- define the macro ABI as `(__CALLER__root, quoted_arg_root...) -> returned_root`; every root is an `AnyValueRef` valid in the current process heap
- add compile-time-only `Fz.Compiler` extern dispatch in the interpreter path, wired to source-production services; normal runtime/native code must not gain compiler authority
- implement quote/unquote over the compiler2 Fz-shaped quoted source value model in-place on the active source heap
- validate/root returned AST values before recursive expansion continues, without decoding them through a Rust AST representation or treating raw heap address as source identity
- reject or diagnose any attempt to use a root that is not valid for the active process heap/world
- define deterministic macro execution limits/signals for recursion/runaway expansion
- add telemetry for macro lowering/execution/service calls carrying raw ids/source-root identities/source facts, not formatted strings

Why:
- the old evaluator proves the semantics, but its value model and whole-program preload are the wrong substrate
- macros are ordinary IR-lowered compile-time functions over Fz data; their only authority is returned source roots and `Fz.Compiler` service calls
title: Compiler2 macro runtime: lower defmacro to IR and run on source heap
